hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will improve SSTR2 selectivity by forming specific π-π stacking interactions with ECL2 residues (e.g., Y193) unique to SSTR2, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will enhance TM6 complementarity and reduce off-target binding.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are strong binders but lack sufficient selectivity over off-target SSTRs. The next iteration should focus on disrupting conserved pocket interactions at non-pharmacophore positions to improve selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: To target non-pharmacophore positions that are likely involved in off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5, particularly in the conserved pocket.
Introduce mutations at key non-pharmacophore positions to reduce off-target interactions while maintaining the integrity of the pharmacophore.
